completed_tasks.name: Retire fever from the documented production path (README)
[BLOCKING] Shadow-run parity gate + Fever cutover (R6)
completed_tasks.progress: Both original blockers (missing embedder, empty infotriage.articles) diagnosed and resolved this session. Ingest re-triggered, 109 real articles now in Postgres. A second real bug (on_message reading item_id from the wrong place) was found and fixed with a regression test, but the fix is NOT yet deployed to the running triage container and NOT yet committed. 0 enrichment rows exist right now.

remaining_tasks.name: Deploy the on_message fix, get >=10 enrichment rows, run shadow_run.py, get operator approval, then crontab cutover

blockers.description: apps/triage/worker.py's on_message() fix + its regression test in tests/test_triage_worker.py are uncommitted. Container still runs the old buggy image (0 enrichment rows).
blockers.workaround: Commit the fix, then `docker compose up -d --build triage` to deploy it before doing anything else.

blockers.description: infotriage.dlq's ~110+ dead-lettered item.ingested messages (from the pre-fix bug) are gone (queue shows 0 messages now, RabbitMQ TTL/policy likely expired them) — those specific historic ingest events cannot be replayed.
blockers.workaround: Not needed: the 109 articles already in infotriage.articles are real and persisted. Once the fixed worker is running, either wait for the next scheduled /run trigger or manually POST /run again on ingest-youtube (:22011) / ingest-imap (:22010) — new item.ingested events will flow correctly through the fixed consumer. persist_and_publish will NOT re-publish for the already-existing 109 articles (R6 idempotency), so re-triggering ingest mainly helps if new content exists; if not, may need to manually publish item.ingested for a handful of the 109 existing article ids to reach the >=10 enrichment bar quickly (read-only re-publish, does not touch data).

blockers.description: DO NOT run `pytest` (bare) or any `pytest -m db_live` test between now and completing shadow_run.py's parity check.
blockers.workaround: See blocking constraint below. Scope any test runs with -k to exclude test_triage_enrichment.py, test_store_integration.py, test_store_contract.py's postgres-param tests, or just run non-db_live subsets (e.g. tests/test_triage_worker.py, which uses InMemoryStore only).

human_actions_pending.action: Once shadow_run.py shows >=10 matching buckets, remove the host crontab fever_triage.py entry (crontab -e)
human_actions_pending.context: R6 prohibition: MUST NOT cut over Fever before parity >= 10 is confirmed. This is the plan's own final blocking human action.

decisions.decision: Registered intfloat/multilingual-e5-large on oMLX by downloading the standard HF safetensors repo into ~/.omlx/models/multilingual-e5-large (not a special MLX-community conversion)
decisions.rationale: oMLX's mlx-embeddings backend natively supports the XLMRobertaModel architecture; the model-id resolver strips an org/ prefix and matches the bare directory name, so worker.py's literal 'intfloat/multilingual-e5-large' request resolves correctly without any repo code change.

decisions.decision: Root cause of empty infotriage.articles: tests/test_store_contract.py, test_store_integration.py, and test_triage_enrichment.py all call _truncate_all(DEV_DSN) unconditionally per db_live test, and DEV_DSN (postgresql://...@localhost:22000/infotriage) is the SAME live Postgres the running app stack uses — not a separate test DB.
decisions.rationale: Confirmed via docker inspect timestamps, pg_stat_activity, and direct code read of the fixture. User chose 'just unblock 05-05 now' (not fix the test-isolation architecture) as the scope for this session.

decisions.decision: Fixed apps/triage/worker.py's on_message() to read item_id from message.headers instead of json.loads(message.body)
decisions.rationale: RabbitMQBus.publish() puts item_id only in AMQP message headers; the JSON body is only {source, source_type, ts}. Every single item.ingested message was dead-lettering with KeyError('item_id') — 100% reproducible, confirmed via live logs (110+ DLQ messages) and a stashed-code regression test that failed before the fix and passed after.

decisions.decision: Terminated Postgres backend PIDs 42 (idle in transaction for 1h13m, unrelated pre-existing zombie connection) and 6878 (the stashed pytest's pending TRUNCATE, still waiting server-side even after the client process was killed)
decisions.rationale: Both were blocking all reads/writes on infotriage.articles/enrichment. Terminating them rolled back the pending TRUNCATE cleanly — the 109 articles survived intact.

next_action: Commit apps/triage/worker.py + tests/test_triage_worker.py (fix(05-05) or fix(05-03), since the bug lives in 05-03's file), then `docker compose up -d --build triage` to deploy the fix, then get >=10 enrichment rows (re-trigger ingest and/or manually publish item.ingested for a few of the 109 existing articles), WITHOUT running any pytest -m db_live in between, then run `python scripts/shadow_run.py` and present the parity table to the operator for the Task 3 checkpoint.
context_notes: This session closed out 05-01 (safe-resume gate recovery), ran 05-02/05-03/05-04 via subagents, hit 05-05's Task 3 checkpoint blocked on two real infra issues (missing embedder model, empty articles table), investigated and fixed both, then discovered a second, more serious bug (on_message header/body mismatch) while trying to actually populate enrichment rows for the parity check. The fix is solid and verified (regression test proven to fail-then-pass, full test_triage_worker.py file green) but not yet committed or deployed to the live container. Be careful not to run any db_live pytest before shadow_run.py's check completes — that's the exact trap that caused the empty-articles investigation in the first place.
